messages.author: OverLord6920
messages.text: I know in arduno you can have master and slave mcu via i2c for expanding inputs and sensors. How would someone go about adding something like that?
messages.content_html: I know in arduno you can have master and slave mcu via i2c for expanding inputs and sensors. How would someone go about adding something like that?

messages.author: Mike WB6KTN
messages.text: Are you looking for additional control processors to service the i/o devices or are you asking if MC sensors could use an i2c multiplexor like https://www.adafruit.com/product/5663 ?
messages.content_html: Are you looking for additional control processors to service the i/o devices or are you asking if MC sensors could use an i2c multiplexor like https://www.adafruit.com/product/5663 ?
embeds_html:
Adafruit PCA9546 4-Channel I2C Multiplexer
You just found the perfect I2C sensor, and you want to wire up two or three or more of them to your Arduino when you realize "Uh oh, this chip has a fixed I2C address, and from what I ...

messages.author: OverLord6920
messages.text: I have a xiao nrf52 board an there are no extra ios for extra stuff except i2c. What can i do to add gps, rotory incoder. Led lights?
messages.content_html: I have a xiao nrf52 board an there are no extra ios for extra stuff except i2c. What can i do to add gps, rotory incoder. Led lights?

messages.author: Mike WB6KTN
messages.text: gps can connect over i2c although it more commonly uses serial. You can probably find i2c versions of rotary encoders. You may need a multiplexer if you have an addressing conflict or need to shift voltages. Beyond that it is hooking them up and writing / finding drivers.
messages.content_html: gps can connect over i2c although it more commonly uses serial. You can probably find i2c versions of rotary encoders. You may need a multiplexer if you have an addressing conflict or need to shift voltages. Beyond that it is hooking them up and writing / finding drivers.
